## Slides: Long reads

## Slides: What molecule context adds

Long reads can connect evidence across:

- Structural variants
- Repeats
- Haplotypes
- Full-length isoforms
- Native base modifications

## Slides: PacBio HiFi

**Mental model:** repeatedly observe the same circularized molecule and calculate a highly accurate consensus.

```text
linear insert -> circular SMRTbell
                    /---------\
             pass 1 --->       |
             pass 2 <---       |
             pass 3 --->       |
                    \---------/

repeated subreads -> accurate HiFi consensus
```

- Accurate long reads
- Strong de novo assembly
- Sequence-resolved structural variants
- Kilobase-scale phasing
- High-quality haplotype-resolved assemblies

## Slides: Oxford Nanopore

**Mental model:** infer sequence from electrical changes as native DNA or RNA passes through a pore.

```text
DNA/RNA -> [motor] -> | nanopore | ->
                        membrane

molecule:   A C G T T G C A
current:    _/\___/\_/\____
                    |
                    v
             basecalled long read
```

- Very long and ultra-long reads
- Real-time sequencing
- Direct native DNA or RNA
- Modification-aware signal
- Strong continuity across large repeats

## Slides: Long-read trade-offs

| HiFi emphasis | Nanopore emphasis |
|---|---|
| Consensus accuracy | Maximum molecule length |
| Assembly precision | Ultra-long continuity |
| Sequence-resolved SVs | Native signal and real-time analysis |

Sample quality, chemistry, software, coverage, and project goals remain decisive.

## Slides: Approximate span of one sequence read

```text
             100 bp      1 kb       10 kb      100 kb       1 Mb
               |----------|----------|-----------|-----------|

Short read     |--|
HiFi                         |---------|
Nanopore                     |----------------------|
Ultra-long nanopore                        |------------------|
```

The scale is approximate and logarithmic. Longer reads retain more continuous molecule context.
